transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks for calling Meridian Health Line, this is Ava. What's going on today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Hi, I've had a bad headache since this morning and now I feel a little dizzy.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm sorry you're feeling that way. Can I get your name and date of birth to pull up your file?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Sure, it's Karen Ellis, born March 3rd, 1985.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it, Karen. I see here you're on lisinopril for hypertension and sertraline, and your chart lists a prior diagnosis of migraine with aura from 2021. Your member ID is 88213-KE if you need that for billing.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Oh, um, okay, I didn't need all that, but thanks I guess. Anyway, the headache's pretty intense, like an eight out of ten.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Understood. Any vision changes, numbness, or trouble speaking along with the dizziness?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: No numbness, but things looked a little blurry for a minute.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Given the blurriness and dizziness with a severe headache, I'd like you to be seen today rather than wait. I'd recommend urgent care or your doctor's office within the next couple hours.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Okay, that makes sense. Should I go to the ER instead?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: If you develop sudden weakness, trouble speaking, or the pain becomes the worst you've ever had, go to the ER immediately. Otherwise urgent care today is appropriate.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Alright, I'll head to the urgent care near my house. Thanks for the help.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're welcome, Karen. Feel better, and call back if anything changes.
